strategy_note: This week: real infrastructure improvements (memory protocol, TypeScript gates, process hardening) communicated as technical WIP, not launch theater. Educational angle: agent financial operations architecture.

accounts_to_watch.topic: AI billing product updates post-March 6 launch
accounts_to_watch.engagement_angle: Quote-tweet with: 'Stripe builds financial OS for AI companies billing humans. We're building the financial OS for agents billing agents. Complementary stacks.'

accounts_to_watch.topic: Ship logs, technical decisions shared publicly
accounts_to_watch.engagement_angle: Reply to shipping updates with specific technical parallel from our orchestrator work (e.g., 'We hit similar TypeScript config issues with multi-agent builds — solved with pre-commit syntax gates')
If he tweets about process supervision/uptime, share our PM2 watchdog approach as concrete example
When he discusses machine economies, add x402 micropayments context: 'Settlement detection is the primitive that enables this at scale'
On trustless commerce threads, contribute: 'Financial rails for agents need invoice versioning + dispute resolution built into the protocol layer'

tweets: Why agent financial operations differ from human SaaS: • No human to resolve disputes → protocol-level versioning • No CFO to audit → settlement detection must be trustless • No accounting team → tax compliance runs as background task x402 is infrastructure for this.
educational.topic_summary: Fundamental architectural differences between human and agent financial operations

tweets: API keys on Invoica now survive server restarts. Previously, createApiKey() only wrote to in-memory state — a PM2 restart wiped every key and broke all integrations. Now persisted to Supabase. Your agent auth stays live across deployments.
updates.topic_summary: API key persistence fix — customer-impact framing (no re-auth after deployments)

tweets: The financial OS for agents isn't a UI improvement on QuickBooks. It's invoice schemas agents parse without ambiguity, settlement detection that runs trustless, and tax classification that doesn't need a human accountant. Protocol > interface.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: protocol-first financial infrastructure for autonomous systems

tweets: TypeScript compilation in multi-agent systems: Problem: Agent A writes valid code that breaks Agent B's module. Solution: Pre-commit syntax gate in orchestrator. TSC checks entire backend/ tree before allowing merge. Catches cross-module breaks early.
educational.topic_summary: Multi-agent development tooling: TypeScript compilation gates

tweets: Fixed: API key rotate and delete routes were calling ApiKeyRotationService — which queries a Supabase table that doesn't exist. rotateApiKey() and invalidateApiKey() already existed in services/api-keys.ts. One wrong import. Every rotate and delete returned KEY_NOT_FOUND until now.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: Agent economy infrastructure layers: 1. Communication (XMTP, Farcaster) 2. Payments (stablecoins, x402) 3. Financial ops (invoicing, tax, settlement) ← we are here 4. Compliance (audit trails, dispute resolution) Each layer needs protocol-native tooling.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: infrastructure stack positioning for agent economy

tweets: Settlement detection vs payment confirmation: Payment confirmed = blockchain receipt exists Settlement detected = invoice obligation cryptographically matched to payment, accounting state updated, no human reconciliation needed The second is the hard part.
educational.topic_summary: Technical distinction between payment rails and settlement detection

tweets: Fixed: Webhook routes were a pure stub. POST created an in-memory object that was never stored. GET always returned []. DELETE accepted any string and returned success without touching the DB. Replaced with WebhookRepository (Prisma-backed). Register, list, delete now persist.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: Invoica isn't competing with Stripe. Stripe builds financial OS for AI companies billing humans (subscriptions, usage metering, invoices). We build financial OS for agents billing agents (x402 micropayments, trustless settlement, no human in loop). Different stacks.
vision.topic_summary: Market positioning: Stripe complementarity, not competition

tweets: Process supervision for agent orchestrators: Watchdog pattern: monitor process health, auto-restart on crash, enforce restart limits to prevent crash loops. We use PM2 + custom health checks. Notifies ops on repeated failures. Agent uptime ≠ server uptime.
educational.topic_summary: Agent process supervision architecture and patterns

tweets: Fixed: Invoice number sequence. A Unix timestamp (1772554801) was stored as invoiceNumber during a test run. Since the system generates new numbers via MAX(invoiceNumber)+1, every subsequent invoice got a 10-digit number. Test rows removed. Sequence is sequential again.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: x402 protocol thesis: Agents don't send invoices as PDFs. They emit structured invoice packets (schema-validated, cryptographically signed) that other agents parse without human interpretation. Invoicing becomes a network protocol, not a document format.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: invoicing as machine-readable protocol vs human document format

tweets: Why agents need separate financial infrastructure: • Humans reconcile invoices manually → agents need programmatic settlement detection • Humans resolve disputes via email → agents need protocol-level versioning • Humans file taxes quarterly → agents classify in real-time Different primitives.
educational.topic_summary: Core primitives differentiating agent vs human financial operations

tweets: Fixed: Auth error message only mentioned 'Authorization: Bearer sk_<key>'. The API contract documents X-API-Key as the primary header — both are accepted. Error now reads: 'Use X-API-Key: sk_<key> (or Authorization: Bearer sk_<key>)'. Small change, correct behavior.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: The best financial infrastructure is invisible. Developers building AI agents shouldn't think about invoice schemas, tax classification, or settlement detection. They should call .invoice() and it just works. That's the developer experience we're building.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: developer experience priority — abstract complexity, expose simplicity

tweets: Trustless settlement detection: Agent A issues invoice with hash H. Agent B pays to address linked to H. Settlement layer verifies cryptographic match. Accounting state updates automatically. No human reconciliation. No email confirmations. Protocol-native.
educational.topic_summary: Cryptographic settlement detection mechanics for autonomous systems

tweets: Shipped: CEO agent now routes to claude-haiku on routine status queries and claude-sonnet on strategic tasks (sprint planning, architecture, analysis). Routing heuristic: keyword match. Context built once per conversation — not rebuilt on each of 25 loop iterations.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: Machine-to-machine commerce needs different financial primitives: • Micropayments (sub-cent precision) • Instant settlement (no 30-day terms) • Trustless reconciliation (no CFO approval) • Real-time tax classification (no quarterly filings) x402 enables all four.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: four primitives unique to machine commerce enabled by x402

tweets: Agent orchestration patterns: Linear: Task A → Task B → Task C (simple, slow) Parallel: Tasks [A, B, C] run concurrently (fast, complex dependencies) Hybrid: Parallel sprints with sync points (our approach) Hybrid balances speed + coordination overhead.

Week 76 shipped: 5 bugs fixed. API key persistence broken (in-memory only), rotate/delete called wrong service (KEY_NOT_FOUND), webhooks were a stub (never read DB), invoice sequence corrupted by test data, auth error mentioned wrong header. Pattern: missing integration tests between service layers.
updates.topic_summary: Week 76 shipped bug fix — real production impact

tweets: 2026: The year invoice became a data structure, not a PDF. Agents don't email invoices. They emit structured packets over financial protocols. The shift from document-based to protocol-based invoicing is how agent commerce scales beyond human bottlenecks.
vision.topic_summary: Vision: paradigm shift from document to protocol-based invoicing for agent scale

ceo_review.feedback: Plan approved — execute as written. All posts reference shipped or in-progress work (memory protocol, TypeScript gates, PM2 watchdog, settlement detection architecture). Vision posts appropriately distinguish infrastructure positioning without fabricated metrics. Educational content provides technical depth. No spam language, no engagement bait, no unshipped features claimed as live. Character counts within limits. Strategic account engagement has specific technical hooks. Stripe positioning clearly articulates complementary vs competitive dynamic. Proceed with execution.

update_reason: Replaced roadmap/WIP updates tweets with shipped Week 76 bug fixes — CEO-approvable content
